summary: This notebook implements a PyTorch-based baseline for the CMI3 sensor data competition, processing raw IMU and thermal/time-of-flight sequences through a custom two-branch CNN-LSTM architecture. It applies extensive time-series augmentations, EMA weight updates, and a warmup cosine scheduler to stabilize training across 5 stratified folds. The pipeline demonstrates how to handle variable-length sensor data, extract domain-specific signal features, and aggregate predictions via model averaging. Readers should take away a robust recipe for multi-sensor sequence classification, emphasizing augmentation strategies, temporal modeling, and reliable cross-validation practices.
competition_flow: Loads raw sensor CSVs, groups rows by sequence ID, applies standard scaling and custom signal processing, trains a two-branch CNN-LSTM model across 5 folds with augmentations and EMA, then averages softmax outputs from all folds for final predictions.
data_reading: Reads train/test data via pd.read_csv, groups rows by sequence_id to form time-series sequences, and uses polars for inference dataframes.
data_processing: Forward/backward fills missing values, applies StandardScaler, pads/truncates sequences to a fixed length (100), and applies custom augmentations (jitter, scaling, motion drift, sensor dropout) during training.
Normalizes inputs using precomputed global mean/std tensors and applies a custom FIR filter to IMU channels.
features_engineering: Computes acceleration/gyro magnitude, jerk, energy (squared), and low-pass/high-pass filtered signals.
Applies a custom FIR kernel to the first 7 IMU channels and concatenates these derived signals as additional input channels.
models: TwoBranchModel (custom PyTorch architecture with ImuFeatureExtractor, ResidualSECNNBlock with SE blocks, BiLSTM, AttentionLayer, and dense layers)
loss_functions: Cross-entropy (implemented as negative log-likelihood during training and F.cross_entropy during validation)
ensembling: Averages softmax probabilities across 5 trained fold models before taking argmax for final prediction.
insights: Heavy time-series augmentations (jitter, scaling, drift, dropout) are crucial for sensor data robustness.
EMA weight updates improve generalization and stabilize training.
Combining CNN feature extraction with BiLSTM and attention effectively captures spatial-temporal dependencies in multi-sensor sequences.
Precomputing sequence-level mean/std normalization and using a custom FIR filter stabilizes training.
